mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1% as of September 2008 reflects ongoing labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises month-over-month, or Rank IC between UNRATE and forward returns exceeds -0.05 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero signals monetary policy exhaustion and credit market dysfunction, triggering a flight-to-safety that rotates capital away from equities and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.0% in August 2008, approaching zero lower bound
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 60-day returns is positive (r > 0.1) during crisis regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below trend, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations. This mechanism operates through forward-looking equity valuation compression and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth rate falls below 0.2%
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns is above -0.02 in absolute value over any 18-month rolling window, or SPX produces positive returns in more than 60% of instances where RSAFS growth triggers below threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines signal reduced manufacturing capacity utilization and forward-looking recession risk. This supply-side contraction reduces equity earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth forecasts.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 5-day forward returns is above -0.04 in absolute value over any 12-month rolling window, or SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline combined with rising unemployment signals a demand-side shock that reduces household wealth expectations and consumer confidence. The joint deterioration in housing demand and labor market conditions produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through reduced consumption and investment.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 900 thousand units annualized AND UNRATE rises above 6.0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 965 thousand in July 2008, UNRATE at 6.1% in August 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where both triggers are activated, or the interaction effect (measured as deviation from additive single-series effects) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment declines combined with rising unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumer spending power. The joint signal produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings compression and valuation multiple contraction.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change is negative AND UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137,473 thousand in August 2008, UNRATE at 6.1%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where both triggers activate, or the joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE deterioration explains less than 8% of variance in 60-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI remains elevated while monetary policy tightens (FEDFUNDS rises), signaling stagflationary conditions that compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth. This regime-specific mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5% AND FEDFUNDS is above 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 219.181 in July 2008, FEDFUNDS at 2.0% in August 2008
regime_hint: stagflation or cost-push inflation with policy constraint
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where both inflation and rate conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient between CPI and FEDFUNDS in predicting forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle or recessionary conditions that reduce forward earnings growth expectations. This always-on trend mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices long-term growth assumptions.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,740.3 billion chained 2017 dollars in Q2 2008
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward returns is above -0.03 in absolute value over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 2% annualized.
